reviews.review_title: Needs Polish, But I’m Glad it’s in VR
reviews.review_description:
Used to play this mega obscure space themed shoot ‘em-up RPG hybrid on my phone all the time! Had no idea it got a VR version.

Now right off the bat the controls are WEIRD. The main navigation interface and dialogue boxes often spawns way overhead and doesn’t seem to fix even if I recalibrate height. And you have to navigate the level select like you would levels.

Once you’re in a level controls continue to be weird. It’s gonna take a few deaths to get a hang of controlling the ship, and aiming is very unforgiving. If there is a way to toggle Y-axis inversion I haven’t been able to find it.

The game generously grants 500,000 credits immediately and I highly recommend going directly to the store to buy the LG-3 High Power Lighting Gun (or something with homing qualities) so that you don’t have to be so precise about aiming. 

After that, it’s been a fun experience. Needs more polish, but there’s some fun to be had here, especially if you like this rare genre.

Excellent game, good graphics, combinations of jewels with wings, engines or weapons, tremendous combat equipment, it becomes immortal if you reach a level of 70%, it has electric ray weapons, blue jewel combination, cruise rays, hornet weapon = combination with ruby ​​for greater fire capacity, missiles, buy the most expensive ones, combination with a ruby, the infinity ship is the best, buy the best wings to be able to equip 6 weapons, an engine and the most expensive wings
I couldn't make sense of this, it looked like it was a bad build sent out that wasn't for consumers, and yet I paid for it. Not ready for sale.
